entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor L., a potential commercial feedstock crop being evaluated for cultivation in Pennsylvania

entities.name: PENNSYLVANIA
entities.description: A US state where sorghum performance and disease response are being evaluated

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Zea mays L., a crop that can be grown in rotation with or alongside sorghum

entities.name: COLLETRICHUM SUBLINEOLA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen that causes anthracnose leaf blight in sorghum

entities.name: ANTHRACNOSE LEAF BLIGHT
entities.description: A disease caused by Colletotrichum sublineola that results in leaf lesions and diminished yield

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of the crop, which is negatively impacted by anthracnose leaf blight

entities.name: FIELD SURVEYS
entities.description: Monitoring the presence of C. sublineola in commercial sorghum fields conducted in 2011, 2012, and 2016

entities.name: FIELD AND GREENHOUSE TESTS
entities.description: Evaluation of performance of 158 experimental lines and commercial hybrids of sorghum

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO ALB
entities.description: The ability of certain sorghum lines to resist anthracnose leaf blight

entities.name: FOLIAR DISEASES
entities.description: Diseases affecting the foliage of the plant, for which resistance sources were sought

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Efforts to develop crop varieties targeted for Pennsylvania and northeastern U.S. climatic conditions

entities.name: NORTHEASTERN U.S. CLIMATIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: The specific environmental and weather conditions of the northeastern United States

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, which provided high-resistance sorghum lines

entities.name: ICSB94
entities.description: A specific sorghum line provided by ICRISAT that exhibited the highest level of resistance to ALB in the field
